text: Swear fealty to Zeus and demand his recognition of your bloodright
outcome: You bend the knee — not in submission, but in covenant. 'Rise,' Zeus thunders. 'Lord of Helm-hallen. Olympus recognizes you.' The words shake the stones. The garrison hears. The village hears. Your father will hear. The King of Gods has named you lord.

text: Question Zeus about the justice of your bastard birth
outcome: The thunder quiets. Zeus regards you with something unexpected — not anger, but weariness. 'Justice? I have seen mortal kings burn cities over questions of birth. Your father is a fool. But you — you are asking the right question. That is rarer than a legitimate heir.'

text: Challenge Zeus to prove his rule is just
outcome: The hall goes silent. The garrison holds its breath. Zeus stares at you for a long, terrible moment. Then he laughs — thunder that shakes the rafters. 'NO ONE has asked me that in three hundred years. You have iron in your spine, bastard. I will remember you.'

text: Ask Zeus to bless your rule with the authority of Olympus
outcome: He places a hand on your shoulder. Lightning courses through you — not painful, but awakening. 'You have the blessing of Olympus. Not because of your blood, but because of your questions. A king who questions is a king who learns. Go. Rule. Prove me right.'
